error_signature: Cannot create a string longer than 0x1fffffe8 characters
title: Error uploading large files to YouTube via Google Play node
symptom: When attempting to upload a large MP4 file to YouTube using the Google Play node, an error occurs stating 'Cannot create a string longer than 0x1fffffe8 characters'.
applies_when: Using the Google Play node to upload large files
not_this_if: Using a different node for uploads
root_cause: The error occurs because n8n attempts to handle large binary data in memory, which exceeds the maximum string length limit in Node.js. By switching to filesystem mode for binary data, n8n can handle larger files without running into this limitation.
fix_steps: Ensure the server has sufficient RAM for handling large files
agent_instruction: Set the N8N_DEFAULT_BINARY_DATA_MODE to 'filesystem' and restart the server.
safety_notes: Ensure your server has enough RAM to handle large file uploads.
